clean_story:
"Fear of losing. Desire to possess.
The word jealousy comes from the Greek zelos, meaning zeal or fervor, as it burns with the same intensity.

Jealousy often flares when someone we value pays attention to another, like a child clutching a toy when a friend picks it up. It’s not just about love; it can strike over friendships, achievements, or even possessions. The brain reacts as if to a threat, releasing stress hormones like cortisol. In animals, jealousy can be observed too: dogs may push between their owner and a new pet. Three related subjects are envy in society, rivalry in nature and emotional triggers."
